rulings.Calendar Matter: Demurrer Of Respondent Gerardo Martinez To Petition Of Giorgio Caffese And Antonio Caffese For Revocation Of Probate Of Will
rulings.Rulings: The demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend for contestants to plead facts supporting their claims. Although the documents of which the Court has been asked to take judicial notice support the conclusion that contestants were on actual notice of the probate proceedings, there is no proof of service on them of the documents concerning the will contest before the Petition to Approve Settlement in February of 2012. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling must advise opposing counsel and notify the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) by 5:00 p.m. Wednesday, October 3, 2012.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order That $243,272.00 Bond Be Posted By Antonio Caffese And Giorgio Caffese To Secure Costs Of Suit Of Respondents...
rulings.Rulings: Denied. Even assuming a reasonable possibility that the moving party will ultimately prevail on the merits against the contestants, any award of costs to the moving party would be discretionary under Probate Code section 1002 rather than mandatory under CCP section 1032, and, in any event, the amount of the requested bond is grossly overreaching. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling must advise opposing parties and notify the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) by 5:00 p.m., Wednesday, October 3, 2012.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Petition For Revocation Of Probate Of Purported Will
rulings.Rulings: The motion to strike is off-calendar as moot in light of the ruling on the demurrer. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling must advise opposing parties and notify the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) by 5:00 p.m. on Wednesday, October 3,2012.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KATHLEEN E. DUBS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondent'S Demurrer To Petition To Revoke Probate Of Will
rulings.Rulings: On August 30, 2012, Mr. Murphy filed a notice of appeal from the order appointing Ms. Scrivani administrator. The current petition to revoke probate raises issues and would require the Court to decide matters embraced in and affected by the order currently on appeal. Therefore, the current petition is stayed pursuant to CCP section 916(a), and the Court cannot adjudicate it or the demurrer to it without interfering with the matters on appeal. Both the petition (currently set for hearing on Ocotber 10, 2012) and this demurrer will go off calendar subject to being renoticed once the appeal is concluded. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling must advise opposing counsel and notify the courtroom clerk (415-551-2702) by 5:00 p.m. on Wednesday, October 3, 2012.
